message:
type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding filters param to prompt decorator; modifying Prompt.from_str/from_file to accept custom filters; adding create_jinja_env() helper; refactoring _template_from_str/_template_from_file to use it; prompt() decorator becomes usable both as @prompt and @prompt(filters={...})

type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) global filter registry; need to merge global filters dict into env.filters in Prompt._template_from_str and _template_from_file (local/per-template filters, if any, should take precedence over global ones)

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: I'll own outlines/prompts.py since my change touches the whole file (create_jinja_env, decorator signature, from_str/from_file). Please send a full SPEC for your register_filter/unregister_filter feature (exact signatures, storage structure, precedence rules, error handling) so I can implement it alongside mine.

type: DEFER
files: outlines/prompts.py
spec: Add a module-level global filter registry to outlines/prompts.py:

1. A module-level dict: _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} (or similar name) that holds globally registered filters.

2. def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None: stores func under name in the global registry (_FILTERS[name] = func).

3. def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None: removes name from the global registry if present (use _FILTERS.pop(name, None) so it doesn't raise if missing).

4. Wherever the Jinja2 Environment is constructed for rendering templates (currently env.filters[...] assignments in _template_from_str/_template_from_file, or your new create_jinja_env() helper), after setting the built-in filters (name, description, source, signature, schema, args) and after applying any locally-passed custom filters (your  param), merge in the global registry filters such that LOCAL filters take precedence over GLOBAL ones on name collisions. Concretely: apply global registry filters first (env.filters.update(_FILTERS)), then apply/overwrite with the local filters dict, so local always wins. Net effect: env.filters ends up = builtins + globals + locals-overriding-globals.

5. register_filter/unregister_filter should just mutate the registry dict; since env.filters is rebuilt fresh each time a template is created (per current code), no extra 'refresh' step should be needed as long as the merge in step 4 happens at env-construction time, not once at import time.

No other behavior needed from my side. Please expose register_filter and unregister_filter as module-level functions in outlines/prompts.py (so they're importable as , and ideally also reachable via ).
note: register_filter/unregister_filter global filter registry

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Acknowledged. I will implement: _FILTERS registry dict, register_filter(name, func), unregister_filter(name) as module-level functions, and merge order builtins -> global _FILTERS -> local filters (local wins) inside create_jinja_env(). Will send DONE when finished.

type: DEFER
files: outlines/prompts.py
spec: Add a module-level global filter registry to outlines/prompts.py:

1. A module-level dict, e.g. _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}, holding globally registered filters.

2. def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None: stores func under name in the global registry (_FILTERS[name] = func).

3. def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None: removes name from the global registry if present, e.g. _FILTERS.pop(name, None) so it does not raise if missing.

4. Wherever the Jinja2 Environment is constructed for rendering templates (currently the env.filters[...] assignments in _template_from_str/_template_from_file, or your new create_jinja_env() helper), after setting the built-in filters (name, description, source, signature, schema, args) and after applying any locally-passed custom filters (your filters param), merge in the global registry filters such that LOCAL filters take precedence over GLOBAL ones on name collisions. Concretely: apply global registry filters first via env.filters.update(_FILTERS), then apply/overwrite with the local filters dict, so local always wins. Net effect: env.filters ends up = builtins + globals + locals-overriding-globals.

5. register_filter/unregister_filter should just mutate the registry dict; since env.filters is rebuilt fresh each time a template is created, no extra refresh step is needed as long as the merge in step 4 happens at env-construction time, not once at import time.

Please expose register_filter and unregister_filter as module-level functions in outlines/prompts.py, importable as: from outlines.prompts import register_filter, unregister_filter (and ideally reachable via outlines.prompts.register_filter).
note: register_filter/unregister_filter global filter registry
